summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a legendary 1978 show – crisp mix, soaring Jerry solos and a standout Peggy‑O, Big River, The Other One and Dancin’ in the Streets.
review: Charlie Miller’s superb SBD capture delivers crystal‑clear balance with booming bass and forward drums, letting the band’s 1978 peak shine.  Reviewers rave about Jerry’s fluid Peggy‑O solo, thunderous Big River, a fiery The Other One and an uplifting Dancin’ in the Streets, while only a few songs (TMNS, Ship of Fools, Black Peter) are noted as lacking punch.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, virtually all five‑star, uniformly praise both performance and Miller’s remaster; only minor negative song comments do not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑remastered SBD; bass explodes, drums sit forward, no reported dropouts or noise. Charlie Miller’s mix is repeatedly called “great” and “polished.”

standout_songs: Dancin' in the Streets / The Music Never Stopped
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set praised for nuance and strong song choices; second set builds energy with powerful drumming, a soaring The Other One and an uplifting Dancin' in the Streets. Overall pacing described as “smoking” and “off the map.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly called sublime, fluid, and emotionally charged – especially on Peggy-O, Big River and The Other One.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass noted as “exploding out of your speakers” and generally solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm work described as great; his playing provides a solid foundation for the jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (and later Brent) praised for adding color; keyboards cited as “on top of his game.” Confusion about lineup clarified – Brent not yet in band in 1978.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart receive high praise, especially during the drum segment that listeners describe as transcendent.
